Metadata.CommitMsg:
Use hakermile GHCR namespace for all image references

Replace ghcr.io/matej-basic/ with ghcr.io/hakermile/ across the Kubernetes
manifests and supporting docs (deploy guide, runbook, image scan report) so
image references match the project's own container registry namespace.
Metadata.Author: Ivan Miklenić <imikle1@algebra.hr>

Misconfigurations.Title: No HEALTHCHECK defined
Misconfigurations.Description: You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.
Misconfigurations.Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: No HEALTHCHECK defined
Misconfigurations.Description: You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.
Misconfigurations.Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: limit range usage
Misconfigurations.Description: Ensure that a LimitRange policy is configured to limit resource usage for namespaces or nodes
Misconfigurations.Message: A LimitRange policy with a default requests and limits for each container should be configured
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Create a LimitRange policy with default requests and limits for each container

Misconfigurations.Title: ConfigMap with sensitive content
Misconfigurations.Description: Storing sensitive content such as usernames and email addresses in configMaps is unsafe
Misconfigurations.Message: ConfigMap 'ticketing-config' in 'secure-event-ticketing' namespace stores sensitive contents in key(s) or value(s) '{"API_PORT", "FRONTEND_PORT", "POSTGRES_PORT", "REDIS_PORT"}'
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Remove sensitive content from configMap data value

Misconfigurations.Title: Root file system is not read-only
Misconfigurations.Description: An immutable root file system prevents applications from writing to their local disk. This can limit intrusions, as attackers will not be able to tamper with the file system or write foreign executables to disk.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'postgres' of Deployment 'postgres' should set 'securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to true
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Change 'containers[].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to 'true'.

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with UID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with user ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'postgres' of Deployment 'postgres' should set 'securityContext.runAsUser' > 10000
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set 'containers[].securityContext.runAsUser' to an integer > 10000.

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with GID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with group ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'postgres' of Deployment 'postgres' should set 'securityContext.runAsGroup' > 10000
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set 'containers[].securityContext.runAsGroup' to an integer > 10000.

Misconfigurations.Title: Restrict container images to trusted registries
Misconfigurations.Description: Ensure that all containers use images only from trusted registry domains.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container api in deployment api (namespace: secure-event-ticketing) uses an image from an untrusted registry.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use images from trusted registries.
